sections.title: Getting started
Talking to Elena
Trying to dig in the mud patch

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day to you, Elena.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: You too, thanks for freeing me.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: It's just a shame the mourners confiscated my equipment.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What did they take?

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: My distillator. I can't test any plague samples without it. They're holding it in the Mourner Headquarters in West Ardougne.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: I must somehow retrieve that distillator if I am to find a cure for this awful affliction. Do you think you could help me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course I can help. Where do we start?

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: I was hoping you would say that. Unfortunately they discovered the tunnel and filled it in. We need another way over the wall.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any ideas?

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: My father's friend Jerico is in communication with West Ardougne. He might be able to help us. He lives in a house south east of here, north of the chapel.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm busy at the moment, I'm afraid.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Fair enough.

sections.title: Talking to Jerico

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Jerico.

lines.speaker: Jerico
lines.text: Hello, I've been expecting you. Elena tells me you need to cross the wall.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's right.

lines.speaker: Jerico
lines.text: My messenger pigeons help me communicate with friends over the wall.

lines.speaker: Jerico
lines.text: I have arranged for two of them to aid you with a rope ladder. Omart is waiting for you at the southern end of the wall.

lines.speaker: Jerico
lines.text: But be careful, if the mourners catch you, the punishment will be severe.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks Jerico.

sections.title: Talking to Jerico again

lines.speaker: Jerico
lines.text: Hello again. Have you spoken to Omart yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Jerico
lines.text: You'll find him at the southern end of the wall. Keep an eye out for them mourners.

sections.title: Searching the cupboard

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't need any more bird feed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Mmm, bird feed! Now what could I do with that?

sections.title: Talking to Omart
Talking to Omart again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Omart
lines.text: Hello. Have you sorted that distraction yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Omart
lines.text: Try using some of Jerico's pigeons. Chuck some bird feed at the tower and then release some pigeons nearby. Jerico should be able to help you out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Omart, Jerico said you might be able to help me.

lines.speaker: Omart
lines.text: He informed me of your problem traveller. I would be glad to help, I have a rope ladder and my associate, Kilron, is waiting on the other side.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good stuff.

lines.speaker: Omart
lines.text: Unfortunately we can't risk it with the watchtower so close. So first we need to distract the guards in the tower.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How?

lines.speaker: Omart
lines.text: I reckon some of Jerico's pigeons might help us out here. Chuck some bird feed at the tower and then release some pigeons nearby. They should provide a nice distraction.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where do I get the bird feed and the pigeons?

lines.speaker: Omart
lines.text: I'm sure Jerico can help you out with that if you ask him nicely.

sections.title: Talking to Jerico after talking to Omart

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Jerico.

lines.speaker: Jerico
lines.text: Hello again. Have you spoken to Omart yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have. He told me to ask you about some pigeons.

lines.speaker: Jerico
lines.text: Pigeons? What do you need my pigeons for?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Omart thought they'd make a good distraction for the guards.

lines.speaker: Jerico
lines.text: Ahh, very clever. Well I'm sure I can let you borrow some, as long as you look after them. Just grab one of the cages from behind my house. You'll want some bird feed as well. I should have some in my cupboard.

sections.title: Throwing bird feed

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I probably shouldn't waste it by throwing it here.

sections.title: Opening pigeon cage
Talking to Jerico after freeing the birds

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Jerico
lines.text: The guards are distracted by the birds, you must go now, quickly traveller.

sections.title: Talking to Omart after freeing the birds

lines.speaker: Omart
lines.text: Well done, the guards are having real trouble with those birds. You must go now traveller, it's your only chance.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, lets do it.

lines.speaker: Omart
lines.text: I'd recommend you wear a gas mask before entering West Ardougne. You don't want to risk catching the plague.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That would be wise. Thanks Omart.

lines.speaker: Kilron
lines.text: Welcome to West Ardougne friend. I'm Kilron. Let me know if you want to go back over the wall.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks Kilron. Do you know where to find the Mourner Headquarters?

lines.speaker: Kilron
lines.text: It's the most north eastern building in the city. Be careful around there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be back soon.

lines.speaker: Omart
lines.text: Don't take too long, those mourners will soon be rid of those birds.

sections.title: Talking to Omart after entering West Ardougne

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Omart.

lines.speaker: Omart
lines.text: Hello traveller. Do you wish to cross the wall?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, lets do it.

sections.title: Infiltrating the Mourner HQ
Attempting to remove gas mask while inside West Ardougne
Talking to Kilron

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Kilron.

lines.speaker: Kilron
lines.text: Hello traveller. Do you need to go back over?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes I do.

lines.speaker: Kilron
lines.text: Okay, quickly now!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know where to find the Mourner Headquarters?

lines.speaker: Kilron
lines.text: It's the most north eastern building in the city. Be careful around there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet Kilron.

lines.speaker: Kilron
lines.text: Okay, just give me the word.

sections.title: Talking to Jerico after entering West Ardougne

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again Jerico.

lines.speaker: Jerico
lines.text: So you've returned traveller. Did you get what you wanted?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Jerico
lines.text: Omart will be waiting by the wall, in case you need to cross again.

sections.title: Talking to Mourner outside Mourner Headquarters

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: Back away citizen. Only mourners are allowed inside the headquarters.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm. I wonder if there's another way in.

sections.title: Inspecting cauldron

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Looks like a nice stew is being prepared. This must be to feed all of the mourners.

sections.title: Talking to Nurse Sarah

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello nurse.

lines.speaker: Nurse Sarah
lines.text: I don't know how much longer I can cope here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What? Is the plague getting to you?

lines.speaker: Nurse Sarah
lines.text: No, strangely enough the people here don't seem to be affected. It's just the awful living conditions that is making people ill.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was under the impression that everyone here was affected.

lines.speaker: Nurse Sarah
lines.text: Me too, but that doesn't seem to be the case.

sections.title: Using rotten apple on cauldron

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Anyone who eats this stew is going to have a bad time now.

sections.title: Opening back door of the Mourner Headquarters

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Eugh, locked. There must be a way for me to get in. Maybe there's something around here that will help me.

sections.title: Talking to Mourner outside Mourner Headquarters after poisoning the stew

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: Back away citizen. Several mourners are ill with food poisoning.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there a doctor nearby? Maybe I can go fetch them.

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: There are no doctors in West Ardougne, just that nurse south west of the chapel. You don't need to worry though, we'll soon have it sorted.

sections.title: Searching cupboard by Nurse Sarah
Talking to Mourner outside Mourner Headquarters while wearing gown

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: A doctor? I didn't think there were any left around here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I heard there was some trouble with food poisoning here.

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: You heard right. Head on in and see what you can do.

sections.title: Attempting to remove gown while inside Mourner Headquarters
Attempting to open trapdoor inside Mourner Headquarters
Talking to Mourner inside Mourner Headquarters

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: Oh dear oh dear. I feel terrible, I think it was the stew.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You should be more careful with your ingredients.

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: There is one mourner who's really sick. They're resting upstairs. You should see to them first.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll see what I can do.

sections.title: Talking to sickest Mourner upstairs
When the sickest Mourner dies
Talking to sickest Mourner again while holding the key

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: Sorry, I'd like to be left in peace.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: A doctor? At last! I don't know what I've eaten but I feel like I'm on death's door.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... interesting, sounds like food poisoning.

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: Yes, I'd figured that out already. What can you give me to help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just hold your breath and count to ten.

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: What? How will that help? What kind of doctor are you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Erm... I'm new, I just started.

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: You're no doctor!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The best I can do is pray for you.

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: Pray for me? You're no doctor... You're an imposter!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There's nothing I can do, it's fatal.

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: No, I'm too young to die! I've never even had a girlfriend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's life for you.

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: Wait a minute, where's your equipment?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's erm... at home.

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: You're no doctor!

sections.title: Opening the gate (when entering)
Searching the distillator crate
Talking to Mourner inside Mourner Headquarters after getting distillator

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Mourner
lines.text: Oh dear oh dear. I feel terrible, I think it was the stew.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You should be more careful with your ingredients.

sections.title: Opening back door of the Mourner Headquarters from the inside
Talking to Elena without the distillator

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: You're back, did you find the distillator?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid not.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: I can't test the samples without the distillator. Please don't give up until you find it.

sections.title: Delivering the samples
Talking to Elena with the distillator

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: You're back, did you find the distillator?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, here it is!

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Great! Now I can finally run these tests.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: I don't understand... the touch paper hasn't changed colour at all...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What does that mean?

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: I'm not sure. My old mentor in Varrock, Guidor, might know though. Could you take these to him and see what he thinks? You'll need to drop by the chemist in Rimmington first to pick up some more touch paper.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Now, last time I went to see Guidor, some one told the guards I was carrying some plague samples. Annoyingly, that means they've started searching everyone going to that part of the city.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Ironically, the guards won't be smart enough to recognise the sample itself, so you should be able to get that in just fine. They'll probably confiscate the rest though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what do I do?

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Well I know the chemist has still been managing to get chemicals to Guidor. Maybe he can help you smuggle the things in.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great, I'll see if he can help. See you soon.

sections.title: Talking to Elena again before visiting the chemist

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: What are you doing back here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I just find it hard to say goodbye sometimes.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: I see... I understand what you mean... But let's discuss that later.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid I've lost some of the stuff that you gave me...

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Are you sure? Looks like you have everything to me.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: That's alright, I've got plenty.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: There you go. That should be everything.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great, I'll be on my way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've forgotten what I need to do.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Go to Rimmington and get some touch paper from the chemist. Once you've done that, you'll need to take those things to Guidor in Varrock.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: You'll probably need to smuggle some of the items in to stop them getting confiscated. If you need help moving them discreetly, the chemist might be able to assist you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll get to it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing.

sections.title: Talking to Omart after returning the distillator

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Omart.

lines.speaker: Omart
lines.text: Hello adventurer. I'm afraid it's too risky to use the ladder again. You'll have to find another way into the city.

sections.title: Visiting the Chemist house in Rimmington
Talking to Chancy in Rimmington before talking to the Chemist

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello! Playing solitaire?

lines.speaker: Chancy
lines.text: Hush - I'm trying to perfect the art of dealing off the bottom of the deck.

sections.title: Talking to Da Vinci in Rimmington before talking to the Chemist

lines.speaker: Da Vinci
lines.text: Bah! A great artist such as myself should not have to suffer the HUMILIATION of spending time where the likes of you wander everywhere!

sections.title: Talking to Hops in Rimmington before talking to the Chemist

lines.speaker: Hops
lines.text: Hops don't wanna talk now.

sections.title: Talking to Chemist

lines.speaker: Chemist
lines.text: Sorry, I'm afraid we're just closing now. You'll have to come back another time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I was sent by Elena from Ardougne.

lines.speaker: Chemist
lines.text: Oh, well that's different then. Must be pretty important for her to send you all this way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: She asked me to pick up some touch paper for a guy called Guidor.

lines.speaker: Chemist
lines.text: Guidor? This one's on me then, I owe him more than a few favours.

lines.speaker: Chemist
lines.text: I should warn you though, getting to him might not be easy if you're carrying any chemicals. The guards are searching anyone heading to that part of Varrock.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh right... so am I going to be okay carrying these three vials with me?

lines.speaker: Chemist
lines.text: With touch paper as well? You're asking for trouble. You'd better use my errand boys outside. Give them a vial each. I must warn you though, they do have a habit of stealing things.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well that's no good. How do I stop them stealing my vials?

lines.speaker: Chemist
lines.text: Just make sure you give them something they have no reason to steal. One's a painter, one's a gambler, and one's a drunk. Not ideal I know, but if you pay peanuts you'll get monkeys, right?

lines.speaker: Chemist
lines.text: Now don't stand around here gassing. This is clearly important to Elena. You'll find Guidor's home in the south east corner of Varrock.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks for your help. I know Elena appreciates it.

sections.title: Talking to the Chemist again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Chemist
lines.text: Oh hello, do you need more touch paper?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Chemist
lines.text: Fair enough, don't forget to talk to my errand boys outside if you need help getting things to Guidor. I must warn you though, they do have a habit of stealing things.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Chemist
lines.text: Okay, here you go.

lines.speaker: Chemist
lines.text: Don't forget to talk to my errand boys outside if you need help getting things to Guidor. I must warn you though, they do have a habit of stealing things.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well that's no good. What if they take my stuff?

lines.speaker: Chemist
lines.text: Just make sure you give them something they have no reason to steal. One's a painter, one's a gambler, and one's a drunk. Not ideal I know, but if you pay peanuts you'll get monkeys, right?

sections.title: Talking to Chancy in Rimmington after talking to the Chemist

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, I've got a vial for you to take to Varrock.

lines.speaker: Chancy
lines.text: Tssch... that chemist asks for a lot for the wages he pays.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe you should ask him for more money.

lines.speaker: Chancy
lines.text: Nah... I just use my initiative here and there.

lines.speaker: Chancy
lines.text: Right. I'll meet you at the Dancing Donkey Inn in south east Varrock. Be lucky!

lines.speaker: Chancy
lines.text: Right. I'll meet you at the Dancing Donkey Inn in south east Varrock. Be lucky!

lines.speaker: Chancy
lines.text: Right. I'll meet you at the Dancing Donkey Inn in south east Varrock. Be lucky!

sections.title: Talking to Chancy in Rimmington after giving him a vial

lines.speaker: Chancy
lines.text: Look, I've got your vial but I'm not taking two. I always like to play the percentages.

sections.title: Talking to Da Vinci in Rimmington after talking to the Chemist

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, I hear you're an errand boy for the chemist.

lines.speaker: Da Vinci
lines.text: Well that's my job yes. But I don't necessarily define my identity in such black and white terms.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good for you. Now can you take a vial to Varrock for me?

lines.speaker: Da Vinci
lines.text: Go on then.

lines.speaker: Da Vinci
lines.text: Okay, I'll meet you at the Dancing Donkey in Varrock.

lines.speaker: Da Vinci
lines.text: Okay, I'll meet you at the Dancing Donkey in Varrock.

lines.speaker: Da Vinci
lines.text: Okay, I'll meet you at the Dancing Donkey in Varrock.

sections.title: Talking to Da Vinci in Rimmington after giving him a vial

lines.speaker: Da Vinci
lines.text: Oh, it's you again. Please don't distract me now, I'm contemplating the sublime.

sections.title: Talking to Hops in Rimmington after talking to the Chemist

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi, I've got something for you to take to Varrock.

lines.speaker: Hops
lines.text: Sounds like pretty thirsty work.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, there's an Inn in Varrock if you're desperate.

lines.speaker: Hops
lines.text: Don't worry, I'm a pretty resourceful fellow you know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll see you in Varrock.

lines.speaker: Hops
lines.text: Sure, I'm a regular at the Dancing Donkey Inn as it happens.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll see you in Varrock.

lines.speaker: Hops
lines.text: Sure, I'm a regular at the Dancing Donkey Inn as it happens.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll see you in Varrock.

lines.speaker: Hops
lines.text: Sure, I'm a regular at the Dancing Donkey Inn as it happens.

sections.title: Talking to Hops in Rimmington after giving him a vial

lines.speaker: Hops
lines.text: I suppose I'd better get going. I'll meet you at the Dancing Donkey Inn.

sections.title: Visiting south-east Varrock
Talking to Guard at south-east Varrock

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Please don't disturb me, I've got to keep any eye out for suspicious individuals.

sections.title: Entering south-east Varrock

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Halt. I need to conduct a search on you. There have been reports of someone bringing a virus into this area of Varrock.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: You may now pass.

sections.title: Talking to Chancy in Varrock before talking to the Chemist

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good morning.

lines.speaker: Chancy
lines.text: Leave me alone. I'm trying to find my gambling buddies!

sections.title: Talking to Chancy in Varrock after giving him the wrong vial

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi, thanks for doing that.

lines.speaker: Chancy
lines.text: No problem. I've got some money for you actually.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Chancy
lines.text: Well, it turns out that potion you gave me, was quite valuable...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Chancy
lines.text: I know that I probably shouldn't have sold it... but some friends and I were having a little wager, the odds were just too good!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You sold my vial and gambled with the money?!

lines.speaker: Chancy
lines.text: Actually yes... but praise be to Saradoming because I won! So all's well that ends well right?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No! Nothing could be further from the truth!

lines.speaker: Chancy
lines.text: Well, there's no pleasing some people. Here's your cut anyway.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You have no idea what you have just done!

lines.speaker: Chancy
lines.text: Ignorance is bliss I'm afraid. Here's your cut anyway.

sections.title: Talking to Chancy in Varrock after giving him honey

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi, thanks for doing that.

lines.speaker: Chancy
lines.text: No problem.

lines.speaker: Chancy
lines.text: Next time give me something more valuable... I couldn't get anything for this on the blackmarket.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That was the idea.

sections.title: Talking to Da Vinci in Varrock before talking to the Chemist

lines.speaker: Da Vinci
lines.text: Bah! A great artist such as myself should not have to suffer the HUMILIATION of spending time where the likes of you wander everywhere!

sections.title: Talking to Da Vinci in Varrock after giving him the wrong vial

lines.speaker: Da Vinci
lines.text: Hello again. I hope your journey was as pleasant as mine.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yep. Anyway, I'll take the package off you now.

lines.speaker: Da Vinci
lines.text: Package? That's a funny way to describe a liquid of such exquisite beauty!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm getting a bad feeling about this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just give me the stuff now please.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You do still have it don't you?

lines.speaker: Da Vinci
lines.text: Absolutely. It's just not stored in a vial anymore.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Da Vinci
lines.text: Instead it has been liberated. It now gleams from the canvas of my latest epic: The Majesty of Varrock!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's great. Thanks to you I'll have to walk back to East Ardougne to get another vial.

lines.speaker: Da Vinci
lines.text: Well you can't put a price on art.

sections.title: Talking to Da Vinci in Varrock after giving him ethenea

lines.speaker: Da Vinci
lines.text: Hello again. I hope your journey was as pleasant as mine.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, as they say, it's always sunny in Gielinor.

lines.speaker: Da Vinci
lines.text: Okay, here it is.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, you've been a big help.

sections.title: Talking to Hops in Varrock before talking to the Chemist

lines.speaker: Hops
lines.text: Hops don't wanna talk now.

sections.title: Talking to Hops in Varrock after giving him the wrong vial

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, how was your journey?

lines.speaker: Hops
lines.text: Pretty thirst-inducing actually...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Please tell me that you haven't drunk the contents...

lines.speaker: Hops
lines.text: Of course I can tell you that I haven't drunk the contents...

lines.speaker: Hops
lines.text: But I'd be lying. Sorry about that me old mucker, can I get you a drink?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I think you've done enough for now.

sections.title: Talking to Hops in Varrock after giving him broline

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, how was your journey?

lines.speaker: Hops
lines.text: Pretty thirst-inducing actually...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Please tell me that you haven't drunk the contents...

lines.speaker: Hops
lines.text: Oh the gods no! What do you take me for?

lines.speaker: Hops
lines.text: Here's your vial anyway.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, I'll let you get your drink now.

sections.title: Exiting south-east Varrock while holding the vials

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I should probably talk to Guidor before leaving the area. Otherwise I'll need to get my vials smuggled in again.

sections.title: Entering Guidor's room before talking to the Chemist

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's someone's bedroom. I'm not going in there without a reason.

sections.title: Entering Guidor's room after talking to the Chemist

lines.speaker: Julie
lines.text: Please leave my husband alone. He's very sick, and I don't want anyone bothering him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry to hear that. Is there anything I can do?

lines.speaker: Julie
lines.text: Thank you, but I just want him to see a priest.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A priest? Hmmm...

sections.title: Talking to Julie before talking to the Chemist

lines.speaker: Julie
lines.text: Oh dear! Oh dear! I don't have time to chat!

sections.title: Talking to Julie after talking to the Chemist

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. I'm a friend of Elena, here to see Guidor.

lines.speaker: Julie
lines.text: I'm afraid that Guidor is not long for this world! So I'm not letting people see him now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm really sorry to hear about Guidor.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I do have some very important business to attend to!

lines.speaker: Julie
lines.text: You heartless rogue! What could be more important than Guidor's life? A life spent well, if not always wisely... I just hope that Saradomin shows mercy on his soul!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Guidor is a religious man?

lines.speaker: Julie
lines.text: Oh goodness no! But I am! If only I could get him to see a priest!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A priest? Hmmm...

sections.title: Talking to Asyff

lines.speaker: Asyff
lines.text: Now you look like someone who goes to a lot of fancy dress parties.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Errr...what are you saying exactly?

lines.speaker: Asyff
lines.text: I'm just saying that perhaps you would like to peruse my selection of garments.

lines.speaker: Asyff
lines.text: Or, if that doesn't interest you, then maybe you have something else to offer? I'm always on the look out for interesting or unusual new materials.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have a spare Priest Gown?

lines.speaker: Asyff
lines.text: Well I do sell them. I don't hand them out for free though. This is a shop not a charity.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Please! It's really important.

lines.speaker: Asyff
lines.text: Well I suppose you can have this old one. It's not in good enough condition to sell.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you.

sections.title: Talking to Julie while wearing priest gown top and bottom

lines.speaker: Julie
lines.text: A priest! thank goodness! My husband is very ill! Perhaps you could read him his last rites?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll see what I can do.

sections.title: Talking to Guidor

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, you must be Guidor. I understand that you are unwell.

lines.speaker: Guidor
lines.text: Is my wife asking priests to visit me now? I'm a man of science for god's sake. I know she means well but it's only a little cough!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: She made out it was something more serious.

lines.speaker: Guidor
lines.text: Well it's not killed me yet. So what do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've come to ask your assistance in stopping a plague.

lines.speaker: Guidor
lines.text: You mean the plague of West Ardougne?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's the one. I have a sample here from your former student, Elena.

lines.speaker: Guidor
lines.text: Elena eh?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, she wants you to analyse it. You might be the only one who can help.

lines.speaker: Guidor
lines.text: Right then, sounds like we'd better get to work!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have the plague sample.

lines.speaker: Guidor
lines.text: Now I'll be needing some liquid honey, some sulphuric broline, and then...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ... some ethenea?

lines.speaker: Guidor
lines.text: Indeed!

lines.speaker: Guidor
lines.text: Look, I need all three reagents to test the plague sample. Come back when you've got them.

lines.speaker: Guidor
lines.text: Oh. You don't have any touch paper. I won't be able to help after all.

lines.speaker: Guidor
lines.text: Seems like you don't actually have the plague sample. It's a long way to come empty handed... and quite a long way back too.

lines.speaker: Guidor
lines.text: Now I'll just apply these to the sample and... I don't get it... the touch paper has remained the same.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's why Elena wanted you to do it, because she wasn't sure what was happening.

lines.speaker: Guidor
lines.text: Well that's just it, nothing has happened. I don't know what this sample is, but it certainly isn't toxic.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what about the plague?

lines.speaker: Guidor
lines.text: This result can only mean one thing... there is no plague. It seems that someone has been lying about all of it. The only question is... why?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well this is worrying. I'd better go and tell Elena right away.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was just going to bless your room and I've done that now.

lines.speaker: Guidor
lines.text: Oh. Goodbye then.

sections.title: Talking to Guidor again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again Guidor.

lines.speaker: Guidor
lines.text: Well, hello traveller. I still can't understand why they would lie about the plague.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's strange, anyway how are you doing?

lines.speaker: Guidor
lines.text: I'm hanging in there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good for you.

sections.title: Talking to Julie after talking to Guido

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Julie
lines.text: Hello there. I fear Guidor may not be long for this world!

sections.title: Return to Ardougne
Talking to Elena after talking to Guidor
Talking to Elena again before talking to the King

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Elena.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: You must go and see King Lathas immediately!

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: You're back! So what did Guidor say?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He ran his tests and discovered nothing. The plague... it doesn't exist.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Doesn't exist? But I don't understand... why?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know, but I think we're about to uncover something huge.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Well I think there's only one thing for it. The king must be confronted!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The king?

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Yes. King Lathas of East Ardougne. He was the one that had the wall built. He was the one who called the mourners in. He must know what's really going on here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I'd better go and speak to the king then.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Be quick. You should find him in the castle.

sections.title: Talking to King Lathas after talking to Elena about the plague
Talking to King Lathas again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day King Lathas.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: And to you adventurer. I assume you're here about King Tyras?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am. What's our plan?

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: Well, as we've previously discussed, my brother is currently gathering strength in the lands west of here. The only known way into those lands is through the Underground Pass.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm just passing through.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: Fair enough. Return to me when you are ready and we will discuss our next steps.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I assume that you are King Lathas of East Ardougne?

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: You assume correctly, but where do you get such impertinence.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I get it from finding out that the plague is a hoax.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: A hoax? I've never heard such a ridiculous thing...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have evidence, from Guidor of Varrock.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: Ah... I see. Well then you are right about the plague. But I did it for the good of my people.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: When is it ever good to lie to people like that?

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: When it protects them from a far greater danger, a fear too big to fathom.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't understand...

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: When my father was king, he ruled over a united Ardougne. But on his deathbed, he could not decide which of his sons should succeed him, so he had the city split into two.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: I became king of East Ardougne while my brother, Tyras, ruled over West Ardougne. My brother was a good man and was great at many things. Being a king was not one of them.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: Rather than looking after his people, he went on numerous expeditions to the lands west of here. On one of these expeditions, he was captured by the forces of the Dark Lord.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: The Dark Lord agreed to spare his life, but only on one condition... That he would drink from the Chalice of Eternity.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what happened?

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: The chalice corrupted him. He joined forces with the Dark Lord, the embodiment of pure evil...

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: And so I erected the wall, not just to protect my people, but to protect all the people of Gielinor. Tyras is king of West Ardougne, sealing off the city was the only way to stop him and the Dark Lord.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: I knew people would never believe the truth, so I had the plague made up as an excuse for the cordon. I'm not proud of it, but it was the only way to keep people safe.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. Well at least I know now.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: While my brother is still at large, I must ask that you keep this secret. We can't bring the wall down until we know he is no longer a danger.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But how do we stop him?

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: You're willing to help? Gods be praised!

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: My brother is currently gathering strength in the lands to the west. If we are to stop him, we must find a way through the mountains. There is an underground pass that should lead through but it is full of danger.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: Return to me when you are ready to face this peril. Until then, I give you permission to use my training area. It's located just to the north west of the city.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: I'll also let the mourners know that you are a friend to Ardougne. You will be allowed to pass through the gates to West Ardougne whenever you please. Now, you'd better be off. You have much to prepare for.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: Do you need anything else adventurer?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why don't I get started right away?

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: Your enthusiasm is to be commended. Very well, if you feel you are already prepared then who am I to stop you.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: As we've previously discussed, my brother is currently gathering strength in the lands west of here. The only known way into those lands is through the Underground Pass.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think so.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: Very well. Return to me once you are ready and we will discuss our next steps.

sections.title: Talking to Elena after finishing the quest
Talking to Elena again after finishing the quest

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Elena.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Hey, how are you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good thanks, yourself?

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Not bad, I do wish you could tell me what's going on with the plague though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So do I Elena. I promise I'll tell you everything once this is all over.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: I hope that's not too far off.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It shouldn't be, don't worry.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Elena.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Hey, how are you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good thanks, yourself?

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Not bad, did you speak to the king?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I did, he admitted the plague is a hoax.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Did he say why?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He did. He asked me to keep it a secret though.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: Even from me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid so. Don't worry though, I'm working with the king to resolve all of this.

lines.speaker: Elena
lines.text: I hope so. Even without the plague, I still worry about the people of West Ardougne.

sections.title: Talking to Julie after finishing the quest

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Julie
lines.text: Oh hello, I can't chat now. I have to keep an eye on my husband. He's very ill!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry to hear that!

sections.title: Talking to Guidor after finishing the quest

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again Guidor. How are you doing?

lines.speaker: Guidor
lines.text: I'm hanging in there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good for you.
